content: Is there a way to allocate memory via kvmalloc() or vmalloc()? I want to allocate more than a page but I don't actually need/want contiguous pages at page granularity.
Actually I think for my use case kmalloc() is fine. But I think kvmalloc() / vmalloc() are useful (maybe even critical) in other circumstances
Perhaps introducing a set of try_new_in APIs for relevant data structures? With those the code can pass in an allocator argument.
Sounds good. Will give it a shot
Perhaps you should wait for Miguel and Wedson to chime in before you spend too much time on this idea. :-)
I do think having the flexibility to choose the allocator is going to be useful and important.
I'm just trying random stuff out anyways. Don't mind if I have to throw it away :)
